rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DANIEL M. POMMON JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/22 for an appearance. Responsive papers were not filed in time deliver a courtesy copy to the assigned Examiner at least 10 Court days before this hearing, as set forth in the Notes dated 12/3/21. Exhibit A of the "Supplemental Declaration" filed on 2/1/22 shows two "2-weeks - Rent" payments on 3/13/20 and on 10/8/20 that have overlapping dates. If varying rent payments were made "due to rent increases", why were two payments on 10/8/20 for $500 each when all of the prior payments and most of the subsequent payments (but for one $1,000 payment without any explanation) were $650? Exhibit B of the "Supplemental Declaration" filed on 2/1/22 still does not set forth who the original payee was for the reimbursements the conservator paid herself on 7/21/20 and 8/31/20. Receipts may need to be filed. It seems unlikely that it took five hours to shop for a toaster oven. The fact that Conservatee could use it "to prepare his food and waffles that he loved" is inapposite. Receipts for bank deposits not indicating who owns the accounts are not helpful. If any further papers are filed, courtesy copies of them must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/28/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VENRICE PALMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/08/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) On page 7 of the petition lines 10-13 it appears to not be complete and has blanks in the petition. The petition must state the assets on hand. If there is a difference between the I&A and the assets on hand the difference must be explained. 2) On the attorney timesheets please explain why there are the same entries on both the ordinary and extraordinary timesheets. See entries on the extraordinary timesheets for 6/14/2016, 6/15/2016, 06/07/2016, 08/05/2016, and 2 entries on 11/23/2016. It appears the extraordinary request should be reduced by these duplicate entries as they do not appear extraordinary. 3) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) a.] 4) The proposed order must state specifically what assets are being distributed only alleging distribute the assets of the estate as follows is not sufficient. 5) There are no exhibits attached to the filed petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/22/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEONARD ESPOSTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/22. Examiner needs more time to complete review of an accounting exceeding the two-year period permitted by this Court as well as the extensive time sheets and for an appearance after the following issues are addressed: No notice was given to Norman Reitz of the "Declaration to Correct" the petition. Have the parties who received electronic service of notice consented to service electronically? (When were such consents filed?) The Attachment to the Proof of Electronic Service filed on 1/24/22 indicates that notice was emailed to Mr. Reitz on 11/13/21, before the "Declaration to Correct" the petition was filed. There is actually no such pleading as a "Declaration to Correct" a petition. The legislature has recognized only either a supplement to a petition or an amendment to a petition (or a fully amended petition), as set forth in CRC 7.3. The administrator with will annexed does not disclose in paragraph 9.B. how much she was authorized on 1/17/20 to spend on repairing the real property. The order dated 1/6/20 authorized a total of $55,256.00, which included up to $5,000 in the event of cost overruns, but the accounting reflects over $91,000 in repairs. Refunds for amounts paid during estate administration (e.g., taxes and utilities) or receipts that were returned to the source (e.g., pension) are not receipts includable in the fee base. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/28/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIRIAM ESTEBANA SOSA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner avers that the "issue" referred to in Article Third, Paragraph 2j of Decedent's will should only share in a 10% interest in the estate, as the will "seems to delineate specific percentages to particular individuals which is specific and clear." However, that section states that "each of said issue to receive a share equal to that of the share to be received by any other beneficiary with respect to division of said 90%..." (emphasis added). 2) Petitioner alleges that Emilia Figueroa's 13 great-grandchildren do not have an interest in this estate, as they are "represented by their parents". No authority has been presented to show that they should not be considered "issue". Executor's declaration filed on 1/18/22 is unclear (it is unknown whether Executor believes that there is restrictive language in the will that affects the definition of "issue"). It is also noted that some of the great-grandchildren were born after Decedent's death. 3) Cash was allowed to exceed FDIC limits. Attorney alleges that proceeds from sale of real property was received on about 9/16/20, which, at that time, a stay-at-home order was in place. Executor has been advised to divide the funds among multiple banks. 4) Executor hired her husband to provide services to the estate without prior court approval (a total of $2,525 at $50/hr to clean up, repair and ready the estate real property for sale). She alleges that the rate is competitive, as he holds a master's degree in structural engineering and has done construction work throughout his career. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OLGA PAULINE DENEGRI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests preliminary distribution to 3 of the 4 beneficiaries of the entirety of their shares and full statutory fees for himself and his attorney. Petitioner states that he will hold back the interest of the remaining beneficiary, Aurelia Woodard, as she post-deceased, and there is currently a Heggstad petition pending to confirm that her interest should be distributed to her trust. It is unclear who is entitled to notice and who must waive bond and accounting in this matter. Also, full statutory fees are not generally awarded until the time of final distribution. 2) Petitioner did not petition to increase bond, despite the I&A filed showing an over $2M estate, even though he was only bonded for $900k. Some bond waivers have now been filed; however, there is no personal representative appointed for a post deceased beneficiary, and there has been no determination as to who the successors in interest are. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DECLARATION OF TRUST OF THE ROMELIA CHAVEZ TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: (1) Petition To Suspend And Remove Frances Vicky Gonzales As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) To Surcharge Trustee'S Beneficial Interest; (3) For Instructions That Frances Vicky Gonzales Not Use Trust Funds To Advance Her Personal Beneficial Interest;
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/08/2022. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/22/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANN DORA CARTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A. and B., and 14.59 E.] No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate. No Receipt of Acknowledgment of Duties and Liabilities. No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License. Notwithstanding the lack of a courtesy copy of the petition, it was quickly skimmed online as a one-time courtesy in hopes of avoiding further continuances. Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. Attachment 8 of the petition is incomplete. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] All courtesy copies, including of a verified supplement by the petitioner addressing the issues with the petition, and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
